body.u: When I left school I got myself a job as a groom, where I looked after horses and ponies I exercised every horse each day, schooling them and jumping them. I was working as a groom for three years until I went to Newmarket to train as a point-to-point jockey.
I started racing after two-three years training. I was racing for 7 years at places such as Choring, Lingfield, Tweezledown, where I met a lot of very nice and pleasant people, including my husband.
I then got married 3 years after I met him, but I didn’t give up horses altogether, because we bought some of our own to look after and exercise.
I had 2 children, one who is now 25 and the other 21.
We moved into the countryside and bought some land on which we built a riding school and I started teaching children and adults to ride.
I retired from that at the age of 57 and I am still living in the same house. I died at the age of 65.
